mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS path lowers the short-rate discount factor and eases financial conditions, so S&P 500 prices rise over the next month when policy is moving down rather than flat or up.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after months with a falling FEDFUNDS trend, or if triggered periods show negative average returns in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above the prior 3-month pace, the market prices tighter policy and multiple compression, which should push S&P 500 forward returns lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above FEDFUNDS by at least 1.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-accelerating periods do not produce below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign reverses and returns are positive in most triggered cases over any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which reduces cyclical earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment rises are not followed by below-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate of negative returns in triggered episodes stays below 55% in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized upturn in RSAFS and HOUST signals broad final-demand acceleration and stronger revenue expectations, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS and HOUST growth does not improve 60-day forward SPX returns versus unconditional baseline, or if the combined signal loses predictive power after controlling for either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in INDPRO reflects weakening industrial output and margin pressure in cyclical sectors, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if falling INDPRO is not associated with lower 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears and becomes positive during repeated manufacturing slowdowns.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth stalls while RSAFS growth remains weak, the economy is losing both income support and nominal spending momentum, so equity returns should be negative over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth falls below 0.15% and RSAFS 3-month growth falls below 0.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint weak-payroll/weak-sales condition fails to produce below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign is positive in a majority of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle labor-market deterioration regime, a rising UNRATE combined with falling PAYEMS confirms income shock and recession risk, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the UNRATE-PAYEMS combination does not forecast weaker 60-day SPX returns specifically in late-cycle episodes, or if the interaction term is not stronger than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: After a policy cut cycle begins, the first month of easing should lift risk assets through lower discount rates and a softer recession probability, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by at least 0.25pp over 2 consecutive months while CPIAUCSL inflation is not accelerating
failure_condition: Reject if first-month easing episodes do not produce positive 5-day SPX returns, or if positive returns vanish when CPIAUCSL is stable and FEDFUNDS is cut.
